_comment: Examples whose gold answer is ambiguous/inconsistent with the question. These are EXCLUDED from 'adjusted' accuracy (raw accuracy still reported). Keep this list conservative and always record a reason + evidence.

disputed.nt-1470: Q asks 'how many teams played on 14 Feb 1976' but gold=7 counts the number of matches/ties that day; 14 (teams = 7 ties x 2) is at least as defensible.
disputed.nt-9465: Q asks 'how does brazil RANK in average receipts per visitor' but gold=1,147 is the receipts-per-visitor VALUE, not a rank.
disputed.nt-11748: Q asks 'what is the highest PERCENTAGE of speakers other than polish' (a number) but gold='Yiddish' is the language NAME, not a percentage. Question/answer type are inconsistent.
disputed.nt-9828: Q asks 'average number of affiliates' — the true mean is 1.14, but gold='1'. Gold appears to floor/round inconsistently; 1.14 is the defensible average.
disputed.nt-8912: Q asks 'which region has more than one format' but gold='United States' only; United States, The Netherlands AND Worldwide each appear with >1 distinct Format, so the gold is under-specified.
disputed.nt-6926: Q 'how many times was War Memorial Stadium the game site' — the Game site column contains EXACTLY 7 rows equal to 'War Memorial Stadium' (weeks 2,4,6,7,8,9,13), but gold='6'. The table contradicts the gold; 7 is the verifiable count.
disputed.nt-1642: Q 'name the only american who did not finish the race' requires NATIONALITY, but the table has no nationality column (cols: Class/No/Team/Drivers/Chassis/Engine/Laps). The answer (Masten Gregory is American) is not derivable from the table — needs external world knowledge.
disputed.nt-517: Q 'does Lafayette or Benton HS have green and grey as their colors' requires SCHOOL COLORS, but the table has no colors column (cols: School/Team Name/Town/County/Enrollment/class). Not derivable from the table — needs external world knowledge.
